key_insight: MIRAGE uses lesion-aware training signals to better preserve breast tumor enhancement when synthesizing post-contrast MRI from pre-contrast slices, improving lesion localization over strong generative baselines.
clinical_relevance: This could help create more useful contrast-like MRI images for downstream analysis when actual post-contrast scans are unavailable, but the benefit depends on the target task and evaluation metric.
method: Residual 2D U-Net for MRI contrast enhancement
Combines reconstruction, perceptual, and asymmetric lesion-focused losses
Adds auxiliary tumor segmentation and frozen post-contrast segmentation guidance during training
evidence_signal: On 301 multi-centre MAMA-SYNTH cases, MIRAGE ranked first on six of eight image-, region-, radiomics-, and segmentation-based metrics and outperformed tuned pix2pix, conditional diffusion, and latent bridge-matching baselines for lesion localization.
watch_item: Because the model is optimized with training-only lesion supervision, its apparent advantage may not transfer uniformly across settings, metrics, or downstream models.
paper_title: MIRAGE: Multi-scale Lesion-Informed Representation with Auxiliary Guidance for MRI Contrast Enhancement
